title: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual soul
display_title: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual soul
wikitext:
[]: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual soul?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Wed Jun 01, 2016 3:45 pm I'm wondering how the series makers envisioned the dummy plug to work? I mean its just a simulation so it does not contain a sole, so even if it could make an EVA move the EVA would not be able to generate an AT field, right? btw: did the EVA-Series even have AT fields? What's written in the wiki ( http://wiki.evageeks.org/Dummy_System ) is that basically the EVA is being tricked to "think" that there is a pilot in and synchronize with it. But that in some way implies a huge lot of agency from the EVA that normally is not the case (except unit 01 which also rejects the dummy plug actually). With the original series plot i.e. that each EVA supposedly contains an own sole (not inly unit 01) and this is a parent of the pilot, this level of agency is somewhat in the realm of plausibility. But its contradicted with the fact that no EVA except 01 displays any agency. Anyhow, it an EVA would be capable of agency, and this agency is an expression of the sole of a human that was intentionally put into it, why the need for a pilot at all. So to say let just Yui & Co. go berserk on the angels without endangering their kids. That all ins sum somehow does not make a lot of sense for me. So what are your thoughts on the subject...

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jun 01, 2016 5:14 pm The dummy plugs have artificial copies of Kaworu's soul. They can control the Evas because they're clones of the flesh of Adam. It's the same way the Rei dummy plug works except using Kaworu/Adam instead of Rei/Lilith.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 01, 2016 10:09 pm TraxXavier wrote: But its contradicted with the fact that no EVA except 01 displays any agency.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Jun 02, 2016 1:22 am > The dummy plugs have artificial copies of Kaworu's soul. Isn't a soule uncopyable, otherwice all the Rei clones would we running around awake. Reichu wrote: The only reason the Evas don't display agency more often is because they're bound in restraints and their central nervous system has been toyed with. As a result, they only act on their own under extraordinary circumstances -- i.e., when the script calls for it. Presumably pilots are used because Seele don't trust women with the wielding of god-like power, and children who can only use that power indirectly are much easier to manipulate and leave less to go wrong.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 02, 2016 1:59 am TraxXavier wrote: Isn't a soule uncopyable, otherwice all the Rei clones would we running around awake.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Jun 02, 2016 2:36 pm Eva-02's core seemed to specifically go after the maternal part of Kyoko's soul, so it's possible that Evas only accept mother's souls in the first place, and Gehirn/Nerv simply worked with this limitation.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 02, 2016 2:51 pm TraxXavier wrote: "maternal part" is not really a properly quantifiable property, now is it?

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Jun 02, 2016 3:01 pm Bagheera wrote: We are told directly (in the CI) that this is what happened, so there's no sense arguing about it. It is what it is, whether we think it makes sense or not.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 02, 2016 3:07 pm TraxXavier wrote: That is not a proper rational standpoint. EVA is not religion, no one is to suicide bomb us for tearing down their dogmas or holy held believes. “Question Everything, Believe Nothing, no matter where you read it, or who said it, unless it agrees with your own reason and your own common sense.”

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Jun 02, 2016 3:14 pm Bagheera wrote: If an authoritative source tells us the maternal part of Kyoko's soul is in Unit 02, and we have nothing in the show to contradict it, the rational course is to assume that, yes, the maternal portion of Kyoko's soul is indeed in Unit 02.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 02, 2016 4:10 pm TraxXavier wrote: But the sources does not tell us that an EVA or even for that matter unit 02 go only or even preferably for the "maternal part", Now do They?

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Jun 02, 2016 4:31 pm Bagheera wrote: I don't see how it could be read any other way, given that that's the part the core grabbed.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 02, 2016 4:36 pm TraxXavier wrote: coming back to the quality of said property, why would an EVA's core go for such an arbitrary set of properties.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 02, 2016 4:43 pm TraxXavier wrote: As I explained you can not derive any general laws from a singular event.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 02, 2016 5:02 pm Bagheera wrote: even though we know for a fact that it contains the maternal portion of Kyoko's soul (and, moreover, only contains that portion of her soul).

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Fri Jun 03, 2016 2:26 am Reichu wrote: The show seems to treat the mother-child thing as a given limitation, rather than an intentional optimization. There is no guarantee that mother-child love is going to be the strongest bond there is; it entirely depends on the people in question. What about siblings? Paternal love? Lovers? Etc.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jun 03, 2016 3:01 am TraxXavier wrote: I consider only what happened in the series/movies as canon, whatever anyone of the makers said is optional and to be discarded if based on the events in the show/movie a more plausible/reasonable theory can be generated.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jun 03, 2016 3:06 am TraxXavier wrote: a Kaworu dummy plug, which presumably can force synchronization on any EVA and thus on any resident soul, it stands to argue that her SEELE could just put completely random soles in.

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Fri Jun 03, 2016 8:19 am Bagheera wrote: (protip: if you're claiming your theories are somehow better than those of the people who actually created the show you're off to a very rocky start. You can believe whatever you like but don't be surprised if no one takes you seriously.)

[]: Re: How can a dummy plug work if it does not contain an actual sole?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jun 03, 2016 8:30 am TraxXavier wrote: Where did I claimed that?
